g.text: > Please explain what skills do, and do it as a song.
The user is asking me to explain what skills do, and they want it delivered as a song. They're asking about "skills" in
the context of this system, which would be the agent skills defined in this codebase. Let me first understand what
skills are by looking at the agent system and how skills are defined.
Let me search for skills-related files to understand the codebase structure.
I'd be happy to explain skills in song form! But first, let me look at what skills are in this system so I can give you
I see this is a complex codebase. Let me search for skill-related files more specifically - looking for documentation or
Now I understand what skills are in this context. Let me also look at the requirements file to get more details about
Now I have a good understanding of what skills are in this system. Let me also look at the skill infrastructure analysis
Now I have a comprehensive understanding of what skills are in the yoker project. Let me compose a song about skills.
Now I have a complete understanding! Let me explain what skills do, as a song:
Skills are modules, not tools, not agents—
You type `/git-commit` with a slash command,
And poof! The skill's guidance is close at hand.
Skills don't execute, they **guide and advise**,
Injecting their wisdom right before your eyes!
They're loaded from Markdown with YAML on top,
Giving the LLM a knowledgeful drop!
A skill has a name and a description so clear,
Optional triggers and tools listed here.
The content's the body—workflows and tips,
Tools? They **do** things—they run and they act!
Agents? They work on their own, that's a fact!
But skills? They whisper, they teach, they suggest,
Injecting their knowledge into every request!
They're loaded and registered—no runtime wrath!
Package plugins make skills modular and pure!
Skills don't execute, they **guide and advise**,
Using `<skill>` tags that the LLM can prize!
So when you need guidance but not automation,
Invoke a skill for best-practice foundation!
And let wisdom flow in a conversational flash!
🎤 *Skills: Context injection for your LLM session!* 🎤
**In short:** Skills are reusable capability modules that inject structured guidance into conversations. Unlike tools
(which execute actions) or agents (which act autonomously), skills provide workflows, best practices, and instructions
through context injection—accessed via slash commands like `/git-commit`.
